codeReview.summary: FCM push notification implementation is mostly complete with proper frontend components (fcm.ts service library, NotificationSettings.tsx, NotificationPermissionPrompt.tsx) and backend push token management. However, there is a blocking bug where the removeFCMToken function sends a DELETE request without the required fcm_token body, causing the backend endpoint to fail validation.

issues.description: removeFCMToken() sends DELETE request without body, but backend endpoint /me/push-token (DELETE) expects PushTokenCreate schema with required fcm_token field. This will cause backend validation error when user tries to unsubscribe from push notifications.

Service worker imports Firebase 9.0.0 (firebase-app-compat.js and firebase-messaging-compat.js) but package.json installs @firebase/messaging ^0.12.25 which is Firebase 10.x. Version mismatch may cause compatibility issues.

Silent push handling in 'push' event listener attempts to use self.registration.sync.register('sync-notification-data') but background sync may not be available and doesn't properly communicate data updates to the frontend app.
Unused import: NextFn is imported from firebase/messaging but never used in the file.
Unused imports: Award imported but not used in component.
Unused imports: X, Award imported but not used in component.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. TypeScript type error was fixed during implementation (requestNotificationPermission return type).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows the worker identified and fixed a type error in fcm.ts line 89 where requestNotificationPermission returned 'unsupported' but the type signature didn't allow it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend push-token endpoints (POST and DELETE) were already implemented in a prior feature (notifications-system). The worker correctly used these existing endpoints rather than creating duplicates.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Backend router shows /me/push-token endpoints exist in apps/api/internal/users/router.py. Grep confirmed no new push-token endpoints were added by this worker.

summary: The FCM push notification feature has a blocking bug where the removeFCMToken function doesn't send the required fcm_token in the DELETE request body, causing backend validation to fail. This prevents users from properly unsubscribing from push notifications. The feature otherwise implements the required functionality including the permission prompt, subscription management, and notification preference settings.
